title: SSE Streaming Response Pattern for LLM/AI Backends
summary: A pattern for reliably streaming LLM/AI responses to clients using Server-Sent Events (SSE). The data flow is: client connects to SSE endpoint → server creates an async generator that yields event chunks → client receives typed events (data, error, done) → server handles disconnection by cancelling the generator. Key requirements: heartbeat or timeout to detect dead connections, cancellation handling to stop resource consumption on disconnect, error events with structured error data, and client compatibility testing across browsers and HTTP clients.
why_reusable: Every AI/LLM backend that streams responses needs this pattern. The async generator + typed events + heartbeat + cancellation handling pattern works across FastAPI, Express, NestJS, and any framework with async/streaming support.
adapter_target: Any AI backend service — implement as an async generator endpoint with SSE event typing.
implementation_notes: Use async generator (Python) or ReadableStream (Node) as the data source
Event types: 'data' for content chunks, 'error' for structured errors, 'done' for stream completion
Heartbeat: send a comment line (': heartbeat') or empty event every N seconds to keep the connection alive
Cancellation: catch client disconnect (asyncio.CancelledError / req.on('close')) and stop the generator
Error events should include error code and message, matching the API's unified error envelope
Client compatibility: test with EventSource (browser), fetch with ReadableStream, and curl
tests_required: Unit test: async generator yields correctly typed events
Unit test: error event format matches unified error envelope
Unit test: generator stops on cancellation signal
Integration test: EventSource client receives all events
Integration test: client disconnect stops server-side generation
Integration test: heartbeat prevents proxy timeout
risks: SSE is unidirectional — client cannot send data mid-stream; use WebSocket if bidirectional is needed
Proxy servers (nginx, Cloudflare) may buffer responses unless properly configured
Connection leaks: if cancellation is not handled, generators continue consuming memory/GPU
EventSource API has no custom headers — authentication must use cookies or query params
do_not_do: Do not concatenate strings to build SSE — use a structured event builder
Do not leave generators running after client disconnect
Do not send error details that expose internal state
Do not forget to test with real proxy configurations (nginx, CDN)
dependencies: none (asyncio + Starlette/FastAPI streaming, or native fetch/ReadableStream in Node)
packaging_impact: Minimal — async generator support is built into FastAPI/Starlette
notes: Derived from backend domain wiki/patterns/sse-streaming-response-pattern.md. The pattern describes the interface and control flow, not a specific implementation.
